_metadata.description: Canvas API — Lessons Learned (Empirical Companion). 18 behaviors the canvas-toolbox discovered through production use that Canvas does NOT document or documents incorrectly. Each lesson cites the issue/commit/incident where it was discovered and the defending tool that handles it. Paired with canvas_api_knowledge.md (Canvas-docs-only — strict source separation).
_metadata.runtime_strategy_rationale: Body ~21KB / 4 ≈ 5,250 tokens — within the 'either' band per runtime_strategy_rules. Set to read_at_runtime per named_override_patterns.selective_load: consuming agents load 2-4 of ~16 knowledge files per audit task. Selective-load defeats embed-cache benefits.
_metadata.version_state: v0.1 untested as a consolidated knowledge file (though every individual lesson IS exercised against production via the defending tools listed in each fact). Per canvas-toolbox 0.x convention, not catalogued in README.md until promoted to 1.0 — promotion gated on a real-course audit run that exercises all 16 lessons. Not yet wired into consuming agents' cross_references.knowledge_files[].

scope: Behaviors Canvas exhibits that are NOT in Canvas's own documentation, OR that contradict it. The 16 footguns the canvas-toolbox has hit and defended against in production, plus cross-cutting patterns that bake the defenses into new tools. Out of scope: documented Canvas behavior (lives in canvas_api_knowledge.md); speculative behaviors not yet reproduced.

facts.term: L1 — Module prerequisites silently fail with JSON payload
facts.definition: PUT /api/v1/courses/:id/modules/:mid with JSON payload setting module[prerequisite_module_ids] returns 200 OK but does NOT persist the prerequisite. Form-encoded payload (data={'module[prerequisite_module_ids][]': id}) does persist. Canvas docs don't surface this asymmetry. Defense: form-encoded for all module nested writes.

facts.term: L2 — Module published state is form-encoded too
facts.definition: Same JSON-vs-form-encoded silent-no-op as L1 for module[workflow_state]. Modules appear published in API responses but Canvas's gating logic uses a code path that respects only form-encoded write. Defense: data={'module[workflow_state]': 'active'|'unpublished'}.

facts.term: L3 — Semester due-date updates fail without lock_at: null and unlock_at: null
facts.definition: PUT /quizzes/:id with only due_at returns 400 when the quiz retains a prior-semester lock_at/unlock_at window. Canvas docs don't surface this constraint. Reading quizzes especially carry prior-semester availability windows. Defense: always send all three together when rolling dates forward — data={'quiz[due_at]': new, 'quiz[lock_at]': None, 'quiz[unlock_at]': None}.

facts.term: L4 — late_policy PATCH returns 403 for instructor tokens
facts.definition: PATCH /api/v1/courses/:id/late_policy returns 403 even with a teacher token carrying manage_grades. Canvas requires admin-level scope. Tools cannot programmatically configure missing-submission and late-submission policies with normal teacher tokens. Defense: canvas_new_course_setup agent documents the manual UI step (Settings → Gradebook); admin-token holders can automate.

facts.term: L5 — Classic quiz points_possible may show 0 after question push
facts.definition: POST /quizzes/:id/questions causes Canvas to auto-calculate points_possible from question sum, but the quiz object's points_possible field is NOT updated. Gradebook shows 0 until a manual UI refresh. Defense: explicit PUT /quizzes/:id {'quiz': {'points_possible': N}} after question push.

facts.term: L6 — Classic quiz two-ID handling in audits
facts.definition: Canvas's two-ID pattern (quiz_id + assignment_id) IS documented (covered in canvas_api_knowledge.md D3). The empirical lesson is the audit-side defense: tools comparing module references to assignment dates naively report false 'not in module' positives. Defense: maintain quiz_id ↔ assignment_id index per course; resolve both directions before flagging.

facts.term: L7 — Discussions use todo_date, not due_at
facts.definition: Documented date semantics (covered in canvas_api_knowledge.md D5). Empirical lesson: PUT /discussion_topics/:id with due_at either no-ops silently or returns confusing 400. Defense: discussion-specific date handling in canvas_sync.

facts.term: L8 — NewQuiz / ExternalTool items can't be content-pushed via REST
facts.definition: New Quizzes (formerly Quizzes.Next) is a separate LTI 1.3 tool, not part of the core Canvas REST API (/api/v1). In core REST a New Quiz appears only as an Assignment shell with submission_types == ['external_tool']: assignment metadata (title, points, dates, module placement) is REST-addressable, but the quiz body (questions/items, settings, stimulus) lives in the New Quizzes engine behind a separate API at /api/quiz/v1/. Classic Quizzes is fully REST-addressable (/api/v1/courses/:id/quizzes + quiz_questions), so Classic syncs cleanly and New does not. Read/write asymmetry (the crux): /api/quiz/v1/ supports reads — toolkit pulls a read-only sidecar of settings+items (_pull_new_quiz_sidecar, quiz_engine:new_quiz) — but has no reliable REST create/write for quiz content. A sync tool can recreate the assignment shell (external_tool link) in the target but cannot repopulate questions; the recreated link points at source-course content absent from the target. Why it matters: module + assignment shells sync and the API reports success, so it looks clean, but the body lands EMPTY in target (students see an entry with no quiz); silent, nothing errors. Detection: classified NewQuiz when submission_types == ['external_tool'] (canvas_sync.py:674); plus ExternalTool/ExternalUrl/sub-headers = NO_PUSH_TYPES. Defense: warn-and-skip NO_PUSH_TYPES pre-write; course_quality_check flags empty/floating items after; read-only sidecar preserved, not re-pushed. Correct remediation: Canvas server-side content migration / course copy / blueprint association, NOT REST item recreation (course_mirror notes New Quiz content must be set manually). Prevalence (rising, Instructure-wide): New Quizzes enabled by default for all users 2026-07-01, enforced 2026-08-15 (Classic phased out, no EOL); some institutions flipped early (BYUI: every course Winter 2026), so this gap now affects a fast-growing share of courses, not an edge case.

facts.term: L9 — Workaround: GET /assignments?include[]=rubric works for student tokens
facts.definition: GET /api/v1/courses/:id/rubrics requires manage_rubrics (documented permission boundary in canvas_api_knowledge.md U3). Empirical lesson: GET /api/v1/courses/:id/assignments?include[]=rubric returns each assignment's rubric criteria inline AND works for student tokens. Limitation: does NOT return RubricAssociation metadata (use_for_grading, purpose) — for those, teacher token + /rubric_associations is required.

facts.term: L10 — Empty modules are a sync artifact
facts.definition: Cascade of L8: when all items in a module are NewQuiz/ExternalTool, the module shell syncs but lands empty in target. Students see a module label with nothing in it — looks like a content gap, is actually a sync-skip side effect. Defense: sync scripts pre-warn operator; course_quality_check flags post-sync.

facts.term: L11 — Blueprint migration reports workflow_state: completed even when items silently skipped
facts.definition: GET /api/v1/courses/:bp/blueprint_templates/default/migrations/:mid returns state: completed regardless of per-section exceptions[].conflicting_changes. Per-section migration-details endpoint records actual skips; migration-level state does NOT. Real incident: ITM-327 S2 skipped 51 of 80 items with migration state 'completed' — no operator alert; discovered weeks later. Defense: read subscriber-side migration-details endpoint per associated course; group exceptions by conflicting_changes type; emit PASS/WARN/FAIL per type (content/deleted → FAIL → lock + resync; points/state/settings → WARN; due_dates/availability_dates → PASS).

facts.term: L12 — A stale .env can silently point a write-capable tool at the wrong course
facts.definition: CANVAS_COURSE_ID/MASTER_COURSE_ID/BLUEPRINT_COURSE_ID are env vars. Canvas API has no notion of 'is this the right kind of course for this operation?' — writes to enrolled section or Blueprint child succeed at the Canvas level but do the wrong thing. Real incident: ITM-327 produced 4× page duplication across master/blueprint/2 sections via stale .env + non-idempotent writes + L15 title-collision auto-suffix. Defense: canvas_course_guard.enforce() GETs ?include[]=total_students + /blueprint_subscriptions; hard-stops (sys.exit(2)) when target is enrolled or Blueprint child unless --allow-enrolled is passed.
citations: Wired into canvas_sync.py, course_mirror.py, blueprint_sync.py, course_quality_check.py

facts.term: L13 — Canvas's UI sync creates -N slug orphan Pages
facts.definition: When a Blueprint sync re-pushes a locked Page into a section that previously deleted its copy, Canvas creates a new page at slug-2 (or -N for repeated cycles) carrying canonical content but does NOT update the unsuffixed slug the module item still points at. Silent split: content goes to -N, navigation stays on unsuffixed; students see stale. Section-side delete blocked while master is locked (403). Defense: 5-point fingerprint detection — (1) A's slug ends in -N, B's doesn't; (2) same title; (3) A's body hash matches blueprint canonical; (4) B's body hash differs from blueprint canonical; (5) A is NOT linked from any module item, B IS. Cleanup deferred to Phase 2.

facts.term: L14 — Canvas's lock-state-only sync can silently revert section page bodies
facts.definition: A locked Page in a section can be overwritten with a body hash that NEVER existed in blueprint's revision history. Migration reports state: completed with zero exceptions. CONTRADICTS Canvas's published docs ('Changed content will always overwrite the existing content in the associated courses for all locked objects'). Observed deterministically 2026-05-20 (incident W01) on the lock-state-only sync path (Blueprint UI sync carrying only lock-state metadata changes, no body diffs). Defense: flag section pages whose body has no provenance in blueprint's revisions[] history; operator warning printed when this fires advising against lock-state-only syncs.

facts.term: L15 — Canvas auto-suffixes a Page URL on title collision
facts.definition: POST /api/v1/courses/:id/pages with a title that already exists silently creates a second page at …-2/-4/-5. Canvas docs don't surface this as a hazard for programmatic clients. Repeated non-idempotent pushes (each --push re-POSTs every page) duplicate the same page on every run. Combined with L12 (stale .env), this is the root cause of the ITM-327 4× duplication. Defense: canvas_pages.upsert_page() — GET /courses/:id/pages?search_term=<title>, filter exact-match client-side; if 1 match PUT; if 0 matches POST; if >1 match route to manual review. page_in_module() guards the module-item link.

facts.term: L16 — Clearing a module-item completion requirement needs the whole object blanked
facts.definition: PUT module_item with data={'module_item[completion_requirement][type]': ''} returns 400 Invalid completion requirement type. Canvas docs describe SET but not CLEAR. Defense: clear with data={'module_item[completion_requirement]': ''} (blank the entire field). Setting still uses data={'module_item[completion_requirement][type]': 'must_submit'}.

facts.term: L17 — Blueprint asset_type vocabulary differs between two endpoints
facts.definition: The same asset is named differently per Blueprint endpoint. unsynced_changes (pre-sync) returns lowercase snake_case (wiki_page, assignment, assignment_group, quizzes::quiz); migration-details (post-sync ChangeRecord.asset_type) returns Rails CamelCase class names (WikiPage, Assignment, Quizzes::Quiz, DiscussionTopic, ContextExternalTool). A lookup table built for one silently misses (returns None, not an error) on the other (#37: presync --suggest-locks punted every lock to UI by reusing #28's CamelCase map on snake_case values). Convenient: the unsynced_changes snake_case values ARE already the restrict_item content_type values (wiki_page->wiki_page), so no translation there — only quizzes::quiz->quiz and context_external_tool->external_tool need normalizing. Defense: two endpoints, two maps — blueprint_presync_check keeps a snake_case _LOCKABLE_CONTENT_TYPE; blueprint_exception_report keeps the CamelCase ASSET_TYPE_MAP; don't share one.

facts.term: L18 — Canvas does NOT update or strip data-api-* enrichment when an <a href> is later swapped
facts.definition: When a Canvas-internal link is inserted into a Page/Assignment/Quiz/Discussion description, Canvas auto-injects data-api-endpoint="/api/v1/courses/{id}/{type}/{id}" and data-api-returntype="Page|Assignment|Quiz|Discussion|…" on the <a> tag. If the href is later swapped (to a different Canvas resource OR to an external URL like video.byui.edu/media/t/…), Canvas leaves the original data-api-* attributes in place — they do NOT track the new href. The link clicks correctly (browsers follow href, not data-api-*), so the bug is invisible at first glance; but Canvas's hovercard/preview UI uses data-api-endpoint and will try to fetch the old target, audit tools reading data-api-endpoint vs href misreport where the link points (false-positive 'still references the old thing'), and description hashes/diffs become noisy. Defense: shared helper lib/tools/_link_metadata.py — strip_stale_link_metadata(html) → (normalized_html, count). Idempotent. Parses href + data-api-endpoint into canonical (course_id, resource_path) form; if they disagree (different resource, different course, or href external) → strip both data-api-* attributes; matching pairs left untouched. Companion find_stale_link_metadata(html) → [findings] powers opt-in course_quality_check.py --link-metadata audit. Writer-side wire-in (auto-normalize on every PUT through blueprint_sync.py / course_mirror.py) is the planned follow-up after the opt-in audit validates on fleet data.

facts.term: P-LL1 — Form-encoded for nested writes (defends L1, L2, L16)
facts.definition: Default to application/x-www-form-urlencoded for any write using the nested parent[child] parameter shape. JSON works for flat objects; for nested, form-encoded is safer. Canvas docs say either works; empirically several endpoints accept-but-no-op on JSON.
citations: L1, L2, L16 lessons in this file

facts.term: P-LL2 — Date writes always send due_at + lock_at + unlock_at (defends L3)
facts.definition: Even when only changing due_at, send all three with explicit null for the others if not setting them. Pre-empts the 400-on-stale-window failure mode.

facts.term: P-LL3 — Idempotent writes via title-existence check (defends L15, prevents L12 amplification)
facts.definition: Every create must check title-existence first. Reference: canvas_pages.upsert_page(). Generalize to any resource where Canvas auto-suffixes on collision.

facts.term: P-LL4 — Startup safety guard for writes (defends L12)
facts.definition: Every write tool wires in canvas_course_guard.enforce() before any mutation. Read tools call it advisory-only.

facts.term: P-LL5 — Plan-first, apply-second with explicit confirmation (defends every L)
facts.definition: Operations that modify Canvas emit a plan, require explicit operator confirmation, THEN apply. --plan default / --apply confirmation-gated. Reference: module_settings_sync.py. Pre-empts amplification by giving operator a chance to abort.

facts.term: P-LL6 — Post-write verification (defends L5, L11)
facts.definition: After any write, GET the affected resource and verify the change took. L5 (quiz points_possible) and L11 (Blueprint migration silent skips) both require post-write reads to detect.

facts.term: P-LL7 — Polite throttling and pagination (universal hygiene)
facts.definition: Use per_page=100 for list reads. Add request timeouts (20-30s). Respect Retry-After headers. Every tool here paginates and throttles politely.

facts.term: Audit Indicators — empirical tag emission catalog
facts.definition: Tag emission catalog mapping observed signals to lessons + defending tools: page_orphan (L13, blueprint_orphan_pages Detector A); page_reversion (L14, blueprint_orphan_pages Detector B + operator warning); floating_item (L8, course_quality_check); empty_module (L10, course_quality_check); quiz_points_drift (L5, canvas_quiz_questions); guard_blocked (L12, canvas_course_guard); migration_silent_skip (L11, blueprint_exception_report); page_duplicate (L15, course_quality_check --files); module_prereq_broken (L1, module_settings_sync); module_item_clear_failed (L16, module_settings_sync).

sources: GitHub issues capturing each empirical finding: #25 (vendored-tool drift + module_settings_sync de-hardcoding + clear-quirk), #26 (Page idempotent upsert), #27 (startup safety guard), #28 (Blueprint exception report), #29 (Page-level integrity audit).
Production incidents: ITM-327 (4× page duplication; L12 + L15 amplification), W01 (lock-state-only body reversion observed 2026-05-20; L14).

Defending tools in lib/tools/: canvas_pages.py, canvas_course_guard.py, blueprint_exception_report.py, blueprint_orphan_pages.py, module_settings_sync.py, canvas_quiz_questions.py, course_quality_check.py, canvas_sync.py.
AGENTS.md External System Lessons table — migrated here 2026-05-21; AGENTS.md retains a pointer.

pre_run_checklist: Each entry in consumed_by[] resolves to an existing agent spec
Each lesson cites at least one defending tool or agent (the in-repo defense)
post_run_checklist: knowledge/canvas_api_lessons_learned.json exists with shape=reference and facts[] non-empty
provenance.sources[] is non-empty (empirical sources only — issues, incidents, defending tools)
MD body has more than 100 bytes past section headers (well exceeded)
No Canvas-authored citations in facts[] — by design, this file documents what Canvas does NOT document

changes: Initial authoring per make_agent_knowledge v1.0 contract. Reference shape (lookup of empirical footguns + cross-cutting defense patterns + audit indicators).

24 facts[] entries: 16 lessons (L1-L16) + 7 cross-cutting patterns (P-LL1 through P-LL7) + 1 audit indicators catalog.
Migrated content: 16 lessons originated as External System Lessons table in AGENTS.md (now consolidated here; AGENTS.md retains a pointer per the architecture decision 2026-05-21).
Runtime strategy set to read_at_runtime via selective_load override (either band).
Version state: v0.1 untested as a CONSOLIDATED knowledge file, though every individual lesson IS exercised against production via the defending tools.
Not yet wired into consuming agents' cross_references.knowledge_files[] — downstream step per P-007.
